text.title: DB Comparison — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: anchor on year-1 scale, weighted scoring not opinion, named fallbacks, named decision owner, version bump on re-decide.

rule.statement: The decision MUST be anchored on year-1 projected vector count + growth rate, not peak vectors at year-5. Migration cost is bounded by year-1 ceiling, not vendor marketing scale.
rule.rationale: Picking Pinecone at year-5 numbers commits the team to managed costs that overshoot a year-1 prototype's revenue. Anchor decisions on the next 12 months.

rule.statement: Each candidate MUST be scored against a weighted criteria list {scale_fit, hosting_fit, hybrid_search, payload_filter_perf, multi_tenant, existing_postgres, team_familiarity}. Weights MUST sum to 1.0; scores are 0..3.
rule.rationale: Opinion-only picks rationalise vendor preference. Weighted scoring forces the team to publish the trade-off.

rule.statement: decision.json MUST list 1–3 fallback DBs in ranked order with a one-line trigger condition each ("if year-1 doubles, switch to Qdrant").
rule.rationale: Without a documented fallback, the team rebuilds the decision under pressure when scale shifts.

rule.statement: decision.json MUST carry decision_owner (role+person) and last_reviewed (ISO date within 30 days).
rule.rationale: Anonymous, undated decisions become folklore that nobody owns when corpus growth changes the right answer.

rule.statement: Any re-decision MUST bump `decision_version` (semver) and reference the prior decision in `prior_decisions[]`.
rule.rationale: Versioned decisions let new team members replay the rationale; loose overwrites destroy that history.
